name: Two Pointers
summary: Start wide; always move the shorter wall inward — O(n).

lineExplanations.3: Get n, the number of vertical lines.
lineExplanations.4: Track the maximum area found so far.
lineExplanations.5: Left wall starts at the first line.
lineExplanations.6: Right wall starts at the last line — the widest container.
lineExplanations.7: Keep going while the two walls haven't met.
lineExplanations.8: Water height is limited by the shorter of the two walls.
lineExplanations.9: Width is the distance between the walls.
lineExplanations.10: Area = height × width for this container.
lineExplanations.11: Keep the larger of the current best and this area.
lineExplanations.12: Compare the two walls to decide which to move.
lineExplanations.13: The left wall is shorter, so move it inward.
lineExplanations.15: Otherwise the right wall is shorter (or equal), so move it inward.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the required wrapper class.
syntaxExplanations.2: def declares maxArea; self is the object, height is the input list.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(height) counts the lines; stored in n.
syntaxExplanations.4: Assigns mx = 0, the running best area.
syntaxExplanations.6: n - 1 is the last index; assigned to rp.
syntaxExplanations.7: while repeats while lp < rp is true.
syntaxExplanations.8: min(a, b) returns the smaller of the two heights.
syntaxExplanations.9: Subtraction gives the width between the two walls.
syntaxExplanations.10: * multiplies height by width to get the area.
syntaxExplanations.11: max(a, b) keeps the larger of mx and area.
syntaxExplanations.12: if compares the two wall heights.
syntaxExplanations.14: else runs when the if condition was false.
syntaxExplanations.16: return hands back the best area.
